So there is a png in test_1. The paper_parsered doesn't have the paper images - they might need to be extracted separately. I'll design the experiment to:

1. Build a script that:
   - Loads the JSON and selects a subset (e.g. abstract + one image block with caption + one paragraph that references the figure)
   - Uses the same image resolution as multimodal_builder (base_dir from paper_id)
   - If image exists: encode and send text+image to Kimi, then ask a question that requires understanding the image (e.g. "根据 Fig.1 的图片和上文，描述这张图展示的内容")
   - If image doesn't exist: we could copy or symlink the test_1 screenshot as a placeholder for one test, and also document that for the real paper the user should place images in paper_parsered/<paper_id>/images/

Actually re-reading the user message: "要验证的是可以根据image_path理解到图片" - they want to verify that the model can understand the image when given image_path. So the experiment is: we have content_list with text and image blocks (img_path); we build the multimodal message; we send to the model; we ask something that can only be answered if the model "saw" the image. So we need at least one real image. Since the project doesn't have the paper images, I'll:
1. Create the test script that uses multimodal_builder to build from the JSON subset
2. Use base_dir that points to paper_parsered - when images are missing, the builder will skip them (with warning). So the test would still run but with text only - we can add a check: if no image was added, print "请将论文图片放入 datasets/paper_parsered/1-s2.0-S0167577X24006608-main/images/ 后重试"
3. Alternatively, for a self-contained demo, we could add a small test image in test_2 (e.g. a placeholder) and a minimal JSON that references it, so the pipeline is verified end-to-end. Then the user can replace with real paper images.

I'll go with: create the experiment that (1) builds from the real JSON subset and real image paths, (2) if images are found, runs the model and verifies understanding; (3) if not found, instructs user where to put images. And add a README for test_2. I'll also add an optional fallback: a test with a local image path (test_2/sample_fig.png) so we can run a quick sanity check - we create a minimal content_list that references that local image and run. So two parts: A) full pipeline with paper JSON + paper images (when available), B) minimal test with one local image to verify the API works.

Let me implement:
1. test_2/README.md - experiment purpose and how to run
2. test_2/build_subset.py or just in test_2/run_experiment.py - load JSON, take subset (e.g. items 0-30 including Fig.1 image), build messages via multimodal_builder
3. test_2/run_experiment.py - call Kimi with the built messages, ask "根据上文和 Fig.1 的图片，请描述 Fig.1 中你看到的衍射花样或结构特征"，parse response and print. Optionally save response to file.
4. Handle missing images: try paper_parsered first; if no images, create a sample image in test_2 (we could generate a simple placeholder or ask user to add one) - actually the user said "你可以节选一下进行测试", so they're ok with a subset. So just document that images must be in paper_parsered/paper_id/ for the figure paths. I'll add a small script to copy or check for images.

Checking the multimodal_builder again - find_image_file(base_dir, img_path) searches base_dir/**/filename. So base_dir = paper_parsered/1-s2.0-S0167577X24006608-main, and img_path = images/78bead8f....jpg. So full path would be base_dir/images/78bead8f...jpg. The folder structure doesn't have images. So for the experiment to run with real content we need to either have the user add images, or use a mock. I'll create the experiment to work with the real JSON and real paths, and add a "dry run" or "mock" mode that uses one local image (e.g. test_1's screenshot) so the user can run it immediately. So:
- run_experiment.py: 
  - Load JSON subset
  - Get base_dir from multimodal_builder
  - Build messages (multimodal_builder.build_multimodal_messages with a custom instruction)
  - If no image in the built content (because all images failed to load), optionally use a single local image from test_2/assets/fig1_placeholder.jpg for one figure to at least test the API
  - Call Kimi with instruction like "请结合上文和图片，回答：Fig.1 这张图展示的是什么？请根据你看到的图片内容描述。"
  - Print and optionally save response

I'll create:
1. test_2/run_experiment.py - main script
2. test_2/README.md - description
3. Maybe test_2/config.json or inline - which JSON file, which indices to use. I'll inline the subset selection (e.g. first 30 items which include abstract and Fig.1).
